 # RGE Handoff Document
 
+> **Snapshot**: 2026-06-08 (ISSUE-335 default clean-release package-set gate): task 93 is implemented. The default clean-release measurement path now has a resolver-backed `DefaultCleanRelease` package set from `tools/Resolve-CleanReleasePackageSet.ps1`: it resolves against `cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps`, excludes `rge-runtime-wasmtime`, `rge-runtime-wasmtime-engine`, `rge-script-host`, `rge-expr-wasm`, and `rge-script-bench`, explicitly includes `rge-tool-wasm-bench` because current metadata shows no `wasmtime` or excluded scripting dependency, and emits `cargo build --release -p ...` with no `--workspace`. `tools/compile-timing.ps1` remains workspace timing by default and uses `-PackageSet DefaultCleanRelease` only for the explicit release gate. Verify/workflow coverage was not narrowed, so explicit script-bench coverage remains visible. No fresh isolated-target measurement was run; the clean-build <=120s budget remains open pending the documented next measurement.
+
 > **Snapshot**: 2026-06-08 (guard parser hotfix after ISSUE-335 abort): task-93 automation filed ISSUE-335, then the guard aborted during the plan gate after the monitor emitted another non-strict object-like verdict (`Invalid JSON primitive: ok`). The parser now accepts exact `ok` / `abort` values when the `verdict` key is quoted JSON or a bare object-like key, and it still rejects prefix/suffix impostors such as `myverdict` or `ok-bad`. Focused `GuardSafetyMonitor.Tests.ps1` passes 60/60 and full `tools/dispatch-tests` passes 420/420. Retry ISSUE-335 after committing this hotfix, removing the partial ISSUE-335 worktree/branch, and clearing the stale `ai-dispatch-running` label.
 
 > **Snapshot**: 2026-06-08 (full-automation re-armed for default release package-set gate): task 93 is the next guarded PR-mode automation item. It implements the candidate C follow-up selected by ISSUE-333: make the Phase 9 clean-release measurement path use an explicit default release package set excluding `rge-runtime-wasmtime`, `rge-runtime-wasmtime-engine`, `rge-script-host`, `rge-expr-wasm`, and `rge-script-bench`; make `rge-tool-wasm-bench` an explicit include/exclude decision; update the documented clean-release command away from `cargo build --workspace --release`; and preserve explicit opt-in wasm/script coverage. No Rust source/tests, Cargo manifests/lock, dependency feature flags, architecture lints, scheduler config, queue/loop publish semantics, or shared target-cache changes are authorized.

No fresh isolated-target measurement was run, so the clean-build <=120s budget is not closed.
+
 > Snapshot: 2026-06-08 (guard parser hotfix after ISSUE-335 abort): the first guarded task-93 run filed ISSUE-335 and scaffolded only a TASK template before the guard killed the child on another monitor parse false-abort (`Invalid JSON primitive: ok`). The guard parser now also recovers exact `ok` / `abort` verdicts when the monitor emits object-like text with a bare `verdict` key, for example `{verdict:ok,reason:"healthy"}`, while still failing closed on longer keys such as `myverdict` and suffix values such as `ok-bad`. Focused and full dispatch Pester suites pass. ISSUE-335 remains the task-93 work item to retry after this hotfix is committed and the partial worktree/labels are cleaned.
 
 > Snapshot: 2026-06-08 (full-automation re-armed for default release package-set gate): `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` now has task 93, the implementation follow-up selected by ISSUE-333. Scope is limited to the clean-release measurement/tooling package set and related docs/status: define an explicit default release package set that excludes `rge-runtime-wasmtime`, `rge-runtime-wasmtime-engine`, `rge-script-host`, `rge-expr-wasm`, and `rge-script-bench`; make an explicit decision for `rge-tool-wasm-bench`; update the Phase 9 clean-release command away from `cargo build --workspace --release`; and keep wasm/script coverage visible through explicit opt-in checks. No Rust source/tests, Cargo manifests/lock, dependency features, architecture lints, scheduler config, queue/loop publish semantics, or shared target-cache changes are authorized.

+# Execution Report
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-335
+AUTHOR: Executor / Codex
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-06-08_10-20-45+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- tools/Resolve-CleanReleasePackageSet.ps1 - new machine-readable DefaultCleanRelease package-set resolver
+- tools/compile-timing.ps1 - added opt-in package-set selection for release build timing
+- plans/BASELINE.md - documented the new default clean-release command and remaining measurement gap
+- Status.md - added current ISSUE-335 status snapshot
+- HANDOFF.md - added current ISSUE-335 handoff snapshot
+- change.md - added chronological ISSUE-335 execution record
+- .ai/dispatch.tasks.md - marked task 93 done
+STATUS: AWAITING_REVIEW
+
+## Task Packet Reference
+
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-335_TASK_2026-06-08_10-01-37+0300.md`
+
+## Pre-Execution Review Consumed
+
+No pre-execution review issued for this dispatch.
+
+## Current-State Claims: Falsification Re-run
+
+- Claim: `tools/compile-timing.ps1` currently builds with Cargo's workspace selector.
+  Re-run before edits: `Select-String -Path tools/compile-timing.ps1 -Pattern "--workspace","function New-CargoArguments"` -> exit 0; found `function New-CargoArguments` and `$args = @($Kind, '--workspace')`.
+- Claim: the root workspace currently has no `default-members` selector to reuse.
+  Re-run: `git grep -n -E '^default-members\s*=' -- Cargo.toml` -> exit 1; no match.
+- Claim: `rge-tool-wasm-bench` currently has an empty dependency table and no direct `wasmtime` entry.
+  Re-run: `Get-Content -LiteralPath tools/wasm-bench/Cargo.toml` -> exit 0; `[dependencies]` is empty. `Select-String -Path tools/wasm-bench/Cargo.toml -Pattern wasmtime` -> exit 0 with no output.
+- Claim: the five mandatory excluded package names and `rge-tool-wasm-bench` currently resolve as workspace package names.
+  Re-run: `Select-String -Path Cargo.toml,crates/*/Cargo.toml,tools/*/Cargo.toml -Pattern 'name\s*=\s*"rge-runtime-wasmtime"','name\s*=\s*"rge-runtime-wasmtime-engine"','name\s*=\s*"rge-script-host"','name\s*=\s*"rge-expr-wasm"','name\s*=\s*"rge-script-bench"','name\s*=\s*"rge-tool-wasm-bench"'` -> exit 0; found all six package declarations.
+- Claim: explicit `rge-script-bench` bench compile coverage is currently visible.
+  Re-run: `Select-String -Path .ai/dispatch.verify.ps1,.github/workflows/bench.yml -Pattern 'cargo bench -p rge-script-bench --no-run','cargo bench'` -> exit 0; found `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` step text and invocation plus `.github/workflows/bench.yml` job name and run command.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+- `tools/Resolve-CleanReleasePackageSet.ps1`: added a new resolver for `DefaultCleanRelease`. It runs `cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps`, validates unique package names, validates mandatory exclusions, includes all non-excluded workspace packages in workspace-member order, explicitly checks and includes `rge-tool-wasm-bench`, and emits JSON/object/command/list outputs.
+- `tools/compile-timing.ps1`: added `-PackageSet Workspace|DefaultCleanRelease`. `Workspace` remains the default; `DefaultCleanRelease` is accepted only with `-Mode build -Release` and uses the resolver to generate `cargo build --release -p ...` arguments.
+
+### Tests
+
+- No test files were added. This revision is a tooling/docs change; no Pester tests were required by the task packet.
+
+### Docs
+
+- `plans/BASELINE.md`: documented `-PackageSet DefaultCleanRelease`, the explicit default clean-release command, excluded package set, `rge-tool-wasm-bench` include rationale, unchanged verify/workflow coverage, and the exact next isolated-target measurement command. It also records that no fresh measurement was run and the <=120s clean-build budget is not closed.
+- `Status.md`: added a top snapshot summarizing the implemented gate and remaining measurement gap.
+- `HANDOFF.md`: added a top snapshot for reviewer/operator orientation.
+- `change.md`: appended the ISSUE-335 execution record.
+- `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`: marked task 93 done.
+

+# Task Packet
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-335
+AUTHOR: Planner / OpenAI Codex
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-06-08_10-01-37+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- tools/compile-timing.ps1
+- tools/Resolve-CleanReleasePackageSet.ps1
+- .ai/dispatch.verify.ps1
+- .github/workflows/tests.yml

+STATUS: OPEN
+
+## Goal
+
+Implement GitHub issue #335 / task #93: add a machine-readable default clean-release package-set gate for Phase 9 clean-release measurements. The new default clean-release package set must exclude the Wasmtime scripting stack from the default release build, update the documented clean-release command away from `cargo build --workspace --release`, and keep wasm/script coverage visible through explicit opt-in checks.
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit
+- `tools/compile-timing.ps1`
+- `tools/Resolve-CleanReleasePackageSet.ps1`
+- `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`
+- `.github/workflows/tests.yml`
+- `.github/workflows/bench.yml`
+- `plans/BASELINE.md`
+- `Status.md`
+- `HANDOFF.md`
+- `change.md`
+- `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`
+
+### MUST NOT edit
+- `Cargo.toml`
+- `Cargo.lock`
+- `**/Cargo.toml`
+- `crates/**`
+- `kernel/**`
+- `editor/**`
+- `runtime/**`
+- `tools/architecture-lints/**`
+- `tools/wasm-bench/**`
+- `.github/workflows/fmt.yml`
+- `.github/workflows/architecture.yml`
+- `.github/workflows/deny.yml`
+- `.github/workflows/powershell.yml`
+- `.ai/*.schema.json`
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`

+- `ai_handoffs/AI_HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md`
+- any Rust source or Rust test file
+- any dependency feature flag or package profile
+- any automation publish semantics
+
+### MAY add new files
+- `tools/Resolve-CleanReleasePackageSet.ps1`
+
+### MUST NOT add new files
+- Any new Rust source/test file.
+- Any new Cargo manifest, lockfile, schema, architecture lint, workflow, ADR, doctrine document, or publish/scheduler automation file.
+- Any new committed measurement artifact under `.ai/**`, `B:\sdk/**`, target directories, or Cargo timing HTML/JSON output.
+- Any helper file other than `tools/Resolve-CleanReleasePackageSet.ps1`.
+
+### Advisory machine-readable scope envelope
+
+<!-- handoff:envelope v1 -->
+MAY_EDIT:
+  - tools/compile-timing.ps1
+  - tools/Resolve-CleanReleasePackageSet.ps1
+  - .ai/dispatch.verify.ps1

+INCIDENTAL_OK: false
+<!-- /handoff:envelope -->
+
+## Deliverables
+
+- A deterministic default clean-release package-set implementation in `tools/compile-timing.ps1`, optionally factored through `tools/Resolve-CleanReleasePackageSet.ps1`.
+- A machine-readable package-set surface that reports at least:
+  - set name, such as `DefaultCleanRelease`;
+  - included package names;
+  - excluded package names;
+  - an explicit `rge-tool-wasm-bench` include/exclude decision and rationale.
+- A generated clean-release cargo command equivalent to `cargo build --release -p PACKAGE_NAME ...`; it must not use `--workspace` for the default clean-release gate.
+- Phase 9 clean-release documentation/status updates in `plans/BASELINE.md`, `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, `change.md`, and, only if touched, `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`.
+- Conditional verify/workflow updates only if the implementation narrows an existing workspace gate. If no verify/workflow gate is narrowed, leave `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`, `.github/workflows/tests.yml`, and `.github/workflows/bench.yml` unchanged.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- The default clean-release package set excludes these five packages from the default release build: `rge-runtime-wasmtime`, `rge-runtime-wasmtime-engine`, `rge-script-host`, `rge-expr-wasm`, and `rge-script-bench`.
+- The Planner decision for `rge-tool-wasm-bench` is to include it in the default clean-release package set unless current metadata proves it now pulls Wasmtime or another excluded scripting package. Rationale: it is wasm-named, but current planning reads show it is a stub wrapper with an empty dependency table; including it keeps the default set "all current non-Wasmtime packages" instead of hiding a package solely by name.
+- The package set is resolved against `cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps`; the implementation fails clearly if an included/excluded package name is missing, duplicated, or both included and excluded.
+- The clean-release measurement command documented in Phase 9 docs is changed from `cargo build --workspace --release` to the explicit default package-set command.
+- Existing invocations of `tools/compile-timing.ps1` remain usable for workspace timing unless the docs explain an intentional, compatible parameter addition. Do not silently remove the ability to run workspace timing.
+- If any verify/workflow command is narrowed from full workspace coverage to the default package set, explicit opt-in coverage is added for the excluded wasm packages. `cargo bench -p rge-script-bench --no-run` must remain visibly present in `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` and `.github/workflows/bench.yml` or be replaced only by an equally explicit command that still compiles that bench package.
+- The implementation does not edit Rust source/tests, Cargo manifests, `Cargo.lock`, dependency features, architecture lints, scheduler config, queue/loop publish semantics, or shared target-cache contents.
+- A fresh isolated-target clean-release measurement is optional. If it is run, record the result honestly against the Phase 9 <=120s budget. If it is not run, record the exact command that should be run next and do not claim budget closure.
+
+## Current-State Claims: Falsification Required
+
+Every negative or "unchanged" premise below must be rechecked by the Executor before relying on it.
+
+- Claim: `tools/compile-timing.ps1` currently builds with Cargo's workspace selector.
+  Falsifying search: `Select-String -Path tools/compile-timing.ps1 -Pattern "--workspace","function New-CargoArguments"` -> planning read found `function New-CargoArguments` at line 194 and `$args = @($Kind, '--workspace')` at line 197.
+- Claim: the root workspace currently has no `default-members` selector to reuse.
+  Falsifying search: `git grep -n -E '^default-members\s*=' -- Cargo.toml` -> exit 1 during planning, no match.
+- Claim: `rge-tool-wasm-bench` currently has an empty dependency table and no direct `wasmtime` entry.
+  Falsifying search: `Get-Content -LiteralPath tools/wasm-bench/Cargo.toml` -> planning read found `[dependencies]` with no entries before `[lints]`; `Select-String -Path tools/wasm-bench/Cargo.toml -Pattern wasmtime` -> no output.
+- Claim: the five mandatory excluded package names and `rge-tool-wasm-bench` currently resolve as workspace package names.
+  Falsifying search: `Select-String -Path Cargo.toml,crates/*/Cargo.toml,tools/*/Cargo.toml -Pattern 'name\s*=\s*"rge-runtime-wasmtime"','name\s*=\s*"rge-runtime-wasmtime-engine"','name\s*=\s*"rge-script-host"','name\s*=\s*"rge-expr-wasm"','name\s*=\s*"rge-script-bench"','name\s*=\s*"rge-tool-wasm-bench"'` -> planning read found all six package declarations.
+- Claim: explicit `rge-script-bench` bench compile coverage is currently visible.
+  Falsifying search: `Select-String -Path .ai/dispatch.verify.ps1,.github/workflows/bench.yml -Pattern 'cargo bench -p rge-script-bench --no-run','cargo bench'` -> planning read found `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` step 5 and `.github/workflows/bench.yml` both running `cargo bench -p rge-script-bench --no-run`.
+
+## Constraints / Non-Goals
+
+- Do not change default workspace membership in `Cargo.toml`; `cargo build --workspace --release` is not a valid implementation of this gate.
+- Do not remove Wasmtime, Cranelift, Winch, Pulley, or scripting dependencies.
+- Do not edit Rust code, Rust tests, benchmark source, WAT fixtures, or Cargo manifests.
+- Do not alter architecture-lint policy, lint exemptions, dependency-auditor policy, publish modes, scheduler behavior, queue semantics, or dispatch packet validation.
+- Do not run `cargo clean` and do not delete or wipe `A:\RustCache\target`.
+- Do not commit isolated-target directories, Cargo timing HTML/JSON, generated metadata dumps, or scratch output.
+- Keep docs forward-only: record the new package-set gate and supersede stale clean-release command wording; do not rewrite older historical measurement rows as though they used the new command.
+
+## Verification Gates
+
+The Executor MUST run and document the result of each applicable gate in the `EXECUTION_REPORT`:
+
+- PowerShell parser validation for every edited `.ps1` file. Use a real parser check such as `[scriptblock]::Create((Get-Content -Raw -LiteralPath 'tools/compile-timing.ps1'))` or `System.Management.Automation.PSParser::Tokenize`, and report any parser errors.
+- `cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps` -> expected exit 0.
+- Package-set proof command -> expected exit 0 and must show:
+  - all included package names resolve in metadata;
+  - the five mandatory excluded packages are absent from the generated default clean-release cargo command;
+  - `rge-tool-wasm-bench` is explicitly reported as included unless the Executor halted due to changed metadata;
+  - the generated cargo command uses `cargo build --release -p ...` and does not contain `--workspace`.
+- If the implementation adds a list/dry-run command, run it and copy the generated command/list proof into the `EXECUTION_REPORT`.
+- If `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` is edited, run its parser validation and either run the gate or explain why the full gate was not practical; in either case run any focused commands added for explicit wasm/script coverage.
+- If `.github/workflows/tests.yml` or `.github/workflows/bench.yml` is edited, inspect the diff and document the explicit wasm/script opt-in commands that remain or were added.
+- If any Pester tests are added under an approved future correction scope, run the focused `Invoke-Pester` command for those tests. This revision does not require adding Pester tests.
+- Optional measurement gate: if a fresh clean-release measurement is run, use a fresh isolated target under `B:\sdk`, do not wipe shared caches, run the new default package-set release command, record wall time / Cargo `Finished` line / budget pass or miss, and remove the scratch target after recording.
+- `git diff --check`.
+
+## Halt Conditions
+
+The Executor MUST halt without committing and request fresh guidance if any of the following occur:
+
+- Implementing the package set requires editing `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock`, any `**/Cargo.toml`, Rust source/tests, dependency features, package profiles, or benchmark source.
+- `cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps` fails or does not contain one of the mandatory excluded package names.
+- `rge-tool-wasm-bench` now pulls `wasmtime` directly or indirectly through metadata, or the Executor believes it should be excluded despite the Planner's include decision.
+- The generated default clean-release command still contains `--workspace`, includes any of the five mandatory excluded packages, or omits a non-excluded package without a documented reason.
+- A verify/workflow edit narrows coverage but does not preserve explicit opt-in wasm/script coverage, including `cargo bench -p rge-script-bench --no-run`.
+- A fresh measurement would require `cargo clean`, deleting `A:\RustCache\target`, committing scratch artifacts, or using a target directory outside an isolated scratch path under `B:\sdk`.
+- Scope pressure appears to change architecture lints, workflow surfaces beyond `tests.yml`/`bench.yml`, dispatch publish semantics, scheduler behavior, or package dependency policy.
+
+## Planner Notes
+
+This task implements candidate C selected by ISSUE-333 after the Pulley/default-build wasm audit. The default clean-release gate is package selection, not a runtime-feature change. Historical Phase 9 rows that measured `cargo build --workspace --release` remain valid history; new status text should state that future default clean-release proof uses the explicit package-set command and that full wasm/script coverage remains opt-in and visible.
+

 2026-06-08 (full-automation re-arm default release package-set gate). Added `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` task 93 as the next selectable automation item after ISSUE-333 selected candidate C. The task is a bounded implementation follow-up for the Phase 9 clean-release gate: define a machine-readable default release package set that excludes `rge-runtime-wasmtime`, `rge-runtime-wasmtime-engine`, `rge-script-host`, `rge-expr-wasm`, and `rge-script-bench`; make an explicit include/exclude decision for `rge-tool-wasm-bench`; update the documented clean-release command away from `cargo build --workspace --release`; and preserve explicit opt-in wasm/script coverage. Updated `Status.md` and `HANDOFF.md` to show automation is re-armed with this finite guarded PR-mode task. **Explicit non-changes:** no Rust source/tests, Cargo manifests/lock, dependency features, workflows, scheduler config, queue/loop publish semantics, architecture lints, shared target-cache changes, default publish-mode change, `cargo clean`, or scheduler arming. **Verification:** `git diff --check` clean.
 
 2026-06-08 (guard monitor parser hotfix after ISSUE-335 abort). The first guarded retry for task 93 filed ISSUE-335 and scaffolded only a template TASK packet before the guard killed the child during the Codex plan gate on another monitor parse false-abort: `Invalid JSON primitive: ok`. The parser already recovered exact quoted `"verdict"` keys with bare `ok`/`abort` values; this hotfix also accepts exact object-like bare `verdict` keys such as `{verdict:ok,reason:"healthy"}` and `{verdict:"abort",reason:"scope creep"}`. The exact-value boundary remains fail-closed for suffix values such as `ok-bad`, and a negative lookbehind keeps longer bare keys such as `myverdict` from being accepted as a verdict key. Updated `Invoke-AiDispatchGuard.ps1`, `tools/dispatch-tests/GuardSafetyMonitor.Tests.ps1`, `Status.md`, and `HANDOFF.md`. **Explicit non-changes:** no Rust source/tests, Cargo manifests/lock, workflows, scheduler config, queue/loop/auto publish semantics, architecture lints, task selection logic, verification gate, or task-93 implementation. **Verification:** PowerShell parser validation passed for both edited `.ps1` files; focused `Invoke-Pester -Path .\tools\dispatch-tests\GuardSafetyMonitor.Tests.ps1 -Output Detailed` passed 60/60; full `Invoke-Pester -Path .\tools\dispatch-tests -Output Normal` passed 420/420; `git diff --check` clean.
+

Added `tools/Resolve-CleanReleasePackageSet.ps1`, a machine-readable `DefaultCleanRelease` resolver that runs `cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps`, validates package names, excludes exactly `rge-runtime-wasmtime`, `rge-runtime-wasmtime-engine`, `rge-script-host`, `rge-expr-wasm`, and `rge-script-bench`, explicitly includes `rge-tool-wasm-bench` because current metadata shows no `wasmtime` or excluded scripting dependency, and emits an explicit `cargo build --release -p ...` command with no `--workspace`. Updated `tools/compile-timing.ps1` with `-PackageSet Workspace|DefaultCleanRelease`; `Workspace` remains the default and `DefaultCleanRelease` is valid only with `-Mode build -Release`. Updated `plans/BASELINE.md`, `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, and `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` to make the Phase 9 default clean-release command the resolver-backed package-set build and to record that no fresh isolated-target measurement was run, so the clean-build <=120s budget remains open. **Explicit non-changes:** no Rust source/tests, Cargo manifests/lock, dependency features, workflows, verify-gate narrowing, architecture lints, scheduler config, queue/loop publish semantics, `cargo clean`, shared `A:\RustCache\target` deletion, or fresh measurement artifact. Existing `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` and `.github/workflows/bench.yml` still visibly preserve `cargo bench -p rge-script-bench --no-run`. **Verification:** PowerShell parser validation passed for edited `.ps1` files; `cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps` exited 0; resolver JSON/command output proved 92 included packages, 5 excluded packages, `rge-tool-wasm-bench` included, and no `--workspace` selector; `git diff --check` clean.

@@ -262,14 +262,15 @@ cargo test -p rge-gfx --release --test gate_a_simple_scene_60fps -- --ignored --
 
 **This entry is a Phase 9 PREFLIGHT — a warm-cache `cargo check` baseline ONLY.** It is explicitly **NOT** a proof that the clean-build or incremental p95 budgets are satisfied, and it does NOT close any §13.3 gate. It establishes the first recorded compile-time reference number for the workspace so future regressions can be detected; the formal clean-build and 1-line-edit incremental measurements are deferred to a future dispatch that owns the target-dir rewarm cost and a dedicated harness script.

+**Candidate C implementation result (2026-06-08 / ISSUE-335):** the default clean-release gate is now a resolver-backed package selection, not a Cargo manifest/default-member change and not a dependency-feature change.
+
+- `tools/Resolve-CleanReleasePackageSet.ps1` is the machine-readable surface. It runs `cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps`, emits `schema_version = clean-release-package-set-v1`, `set_name = DefaultCleanRelease`, included package names, excluded package names, the generated cargo arguments/command, and validation booleans.
+- The default clean-release excluded set is exactly `rge-runtime-wasmtime`, `rge-runtime-wasmtime-engine`, `rge-script-host`, `rge-expr-wasm`, and `rge-script-bench`.
+- `rge-tool-wasm-bench` is explicitly **included**. Rationale: current metadata shows no dependency on `wasmtime` or the excluded scripting packages, so excluding it solely because its name contains `wasm` would hide a current non-Wasmtime package from the default release set.
+- `tools/compile-timing.ps1 -Mode build -Release -PackageSet DefaultCleanRelease` resolves that set and generates a command shaped as `cargo build --release -p PACKAGE_NAME ...`; the generated command does not contain `--workspace`.
+- Existing `tools/compile-timing.ps1` invocations remain workspace timing by default (`-PackageSet Workspace`), so warm/full workspace check/build measurements are still available.
+- `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`, `.github/workflows/tests.yml`, and `.github/workflows/bench.yml` were not narrowed. The repository's normal verify/workflow test coverage remains workspace-wide, and the explicit wasm/script opt-in bench compile remains visible as `cargo bench -p rge-script-bench --no-run`.
